title: Project and Import/Export CLI

story.i_want_to: create and manage projects, and import/export user story JSON structures via the CLI
story.so_that: I can bootstrap projects from existing user story files and export project data for backup or migration

rationale: Project creation and import/export are the core setup operations for loopctl. The CLI import command is particularly important because it reads the epic_*/us_*.json directory structure that teams already use for user story files, assembles the import payload, and sends it to the server in a single transaction. Export provides round-trip fidelity for backup, migration, or editing workflows. These commands bridge the gap between local file-based story management and the loopctl server.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl project create NAME --repo URL' calls POST /api/v1/projects and displays the created project
'loopctl project list' calls GET /api/v1/projects and displays all projects with id, name, slug, repo_url, and status
'loopctl project info PROJECT' calls GET /api/v1/projects/:id and displays the project detail including epic/story counts and progress summary
'loopctl project archive PROJECT' calls DELETE /api/v1/projects/:id (soft archive) and confirms the project was archived

'loopctl import PATH --project PROJECT' recursively reads all epic_*/us_*.json files from the specified directory path, assembles the import payload with epics, stories, and dependencies, and calls POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import
The import command validates the JSON structure of each story file before sending and reports per-file parsing errors clearly
The import command resolves cross-epic story dependencies (e.g., story '14.1' depends on story '1.1') by matching story numbers across all loaded files
'loopctl export --project PROJECT' calls GET /api/v1/projects/:id/export and outputs the full project JSON to stdout by default
'loopctl export --project PROJECT --dir PATH' writes the exported data as a directory structure with epic_*/us_*.json files matching the import format
All project and import/export commands respect the --format flag (json, human, csv)

test_cases.name: Project create sends correct payload
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/projects with {name: 'my-project', repo_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'} and returns 201
expected_results: POST request is sent with correct name and repo_url
Output includes the created project id, name, and slug

test_cases.name: Import reads directory structure and assembles payload
preconditions: Temporary directory with structure: epic_1_foundation/us_1.1.json, epic_1_foundation/us_1.2.json, epic_2_auth/us_2.1.json
Each JSON file contains valid story data with id, title, dependencies, etc.
Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import and returns 200 with summary
expected_results: POST request payload contains 2 epics extracted from directory names
Epic 1 contains 2 stories, Epic 2 contains 1 story
Story data includes title, acceptance_criteria, estimated_hours from the JSON files
Output displays import summary: N epics, M stories created

test_cases.name: Import resolves cross-epic dependencies
preconditions: Temporary directory with epic_1_foundation/us_1.1.json and epic_14_cli/us_14.1.json
us_14.1.json has dependencies: ['US-1.1']
Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import and returns 200
expected_results: POST request payload includes story dependency: story 14.1 depends_on_stories includes reference to story 1.1
Dependencies are resolved by story number across epics

test_cases.name: Import reports invalid JSON files clearly
preconditions: Temporary directory with epic_1_foundation/us_1.1.json containing invalid JSON
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} identifying the file with invalid JSON
Error message includes the file path and parse error details
No API call is made

test_cases.name: Export to stdout outputs valid JSON
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/projects/:id/export with project data containing epics and stories
expected_results: Output is valid JSON parseable by Jason.decode!/1
JSON contains epics array with nested stories

test_cases.name: Export to directory writes epic_*/us_*.json files
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/projects/:id/export with 2 epics, 3 stories total
Temporary output directory
expected_results: Directory structure created: epic_1_*/us_*.json files for each story
Each story JSON file contains the full story data
Output confirms N files written to the directory

test_cases.name: Project list displays projects in table format
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/projects with a list of 2 projects
expected_results: Output contains a table with columns: ID, Name, Repo, Status
Table shows 2 rows

test_cases.name: Project archive sends DELETE and confirms
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects DELETE /api/v1/projects/:id and returns 200
expected_results: DELETE request is sent to /api/v1/projects/:id
Output confirms the project was archived

technical_notes: The import command should use File.ls!/1 and Path.wildcard/1 to find epic_* directories and us_*.json files within them
Epic metadata (number, title, phase) should be extracted from the directory name pattern and from the story files' epic fields
Story number extraction: parse the 'id' field (e.g., 'US-14.1') to get the story number '14.1' for the API payload
Cross-epic dependency resolution: build a map of story_number -> story_data across all files, then resolve 'dependencies' arrays to story numbers
Export --dir should sanitize epic titles for directory names (lowercase, underscores, no special chars)
The PROJECT argument should accept either a project UUID or slug — try UUID first, then look up by slug via the list endpoint
Import should handle both flat JSON file input (single file with full payload) and directory structure input (epic_*/us_*.json)
